---
title: "Research"
page-layout: full
---

## Working Papers

**Incentive Design on Networks** (with A. Daripa and S. Kapur)
<br> We design a mechanism for network formation with privately known productivities that implements the socially optimal network through transfers based on opportunity costs, ensuring ex post incentive compatibility, pairwise stability, and budget balance.
<br>[[SSRN]](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4312506)

---

**Network Structures and Credit Rating** (with A. Daripa and S. Kapur)
<br> We study whether productivity-consistent credit ratings can be inferred from endogenous network structure, showing that under suitable link costs equilibrium networks separate types and sustain ratings aligned with productivity.
<br>[[SSRN]](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3446432)

---

## Published Papers

**Decentralized Defence of a (Directed) Network Structure**, *Defence and Peace Economics*, 2020.
<br> I analyse how agents in a directed network allocate defence resources under external threats, showing that decentralised choices can be efficient with a strategic attacker but generally diverge from the social optimum under random attacks.

---

**Centrality in Trade Networks and Investment in Security** (with V. Bove and L. Elia), *Peace Economics, Peace Science and Public Policy*, 2016.
<br> We show that a country’s position in global trade networks—measured by network centrality—systematically shapes and constrains its military spending decisions.
